title: Solid Geometry
blurb: Unit-by-unit practice for the Solid Geometry course — lines and planes in space through spheres and similar solids.

units.title: Lines & Planes in Space
units.blurb: The rules of 3D: what determines a plane, skew lines, perpendicularity, and the two angles every exam measures.

units.title: Prisms & the Cube
units.blurb: The box family: prism anatomy, the cube's √2 and √3 diagonals, and surface & volume you can see face by face.

units.title: Pyramids
units.blurb: Height, apothem, slant, edge — the two Pythagorean triangles inside every pyramid, the ⅓ in the volume, and the frustum.

units.title: Cylinders & Cones
units.blurb: The round solids you can unroll: the cylinder's rectangle label, the cone's pie-slice development, and both volumes.

units.title: Spheres
units.blurb: Every slice a circle (r² = R² − d²), the surface of exactly four great circles, and Archimedes' tombstone 2:3.

units.title: Cross-Sections & Similar Solids
units.blurb: Predict any cut, scale with k / k² / k³, glue and drill combined solids — and the five-triangle exam strategy.

forms.title: The space diagonal
forms.skill: d² = l² + w² + h² — all three dimensions enter.

variants.statement: Find the space diagonal of a $1 \times 2 \times 2$ box.
variants.explanation: $d = \sqrt{l^2 + w^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{1 + 4 + 4} = \sqrt{9} = 3$ — Pythagoras across the floor, then up.

variants.statement: Find the space diagonal of a $2 \times 4 \times 4$ box.
variants.explanation: $d = \sqrt{l^2 + w^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{4 + 16 + 16} = \sqrt{36} = 6$ — Pythagoras across the floor, then up.

variants.statement: Find the space diagonal of a $2 \times 3 \times 6$ box.
variants.explanation: $d = \sqrt{l^2 + w^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{4 + 9 + 36} = \sqrt{49} = 7$ — Pythagoras across the floor, then up.

variants.statement: Find the space diagonal of a $1 \times 4 \times 8$ box.
variants.explanation: $d = \sqrt{l^2 + w^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{1 + 16 + 64} = \sqrt{81} = 9$ — Pythagoras across the floor, then up.

variants.statement: Find the space diagonal of a $3 \times 6 \times 6$ box.
